goal: 重写 Mainline skill，使其替代 AGENTS.md 和 hooks 成为完整 agent 入口

summary.title: Mainline skill as the primary agent workflow entry point
summary.what: Rewrote skills/mainline/SKILL.md so it presents Mainline as a complete agent integration rather than an internal distribution note. The skill now covers trigger policy, CLI installation and setup responsibility, optional hooks, task start, intent-first code reading, editing, commits, sealing, publishing, review/conflict checks, coverage rescue, and skill distribution.
summary.why: The previous skill overemphasized the private internal install loop and did not adequately replace AGENTS.md or hooks as the mechanism that teaches agents how to use Mainline. The rewrite makes the skill itself the durable behavior contract while treating hooks as optional enhancement and private install details as temporary scaffolding.
summary.user_goal: 这个 skill 的功能是替代 agents.md 以及 hook 的能力，教 agent 怎么去使用 mainline，感觉你写的有很大问题啊。包括可能安装 hook 也提供为可选项，hook 应该是增强而不是必选项。有些用户只知道 skill，他不会自己安装 mainline CLI 然后再用命令去 set up，所以你必须保证这个 skill 就包括了所有 mainline 能力，以及尽量能让 agent 去触发，skill 触发的方式（可能也就是 description）怎么写也是值得认真考虑的，因为要像 agetns.md 那样做到保证 agent 的触发率，比如是不是每次改代码都要触发？提交代码要干什么？所有这些东西你都得考虑好啊，你现在写的很简陋了。内循环的内容只是现状附加的一小部分，后面上线后可能要删掉的，感觉你写的这个 skill 只有内循环的作用

decisions.chose: Make the frontmatter description broad enough to trigger on coding, editing, refactoring, commits, pushes, PRs, conflict review, and Mainline setup in Git repositories that use or may need Mainline.
decisions.rationale: The skill must replace AGENTS.md/hook text as the primary agent entry point, so the trigger cannot be limited to explicit Mainline questions or private installation.

decisions.chose: Tell agents not to assume the user installed or initialized Mainline, and include CLI availability checks, internal Go bootstrap, status verification, and initialization/doctor flow when setup is requested.
decisions.rationale: A user who only installs the skill may expect the agent to bootstrap the rest of the Mainline workflow.

decisions.chose: Define hooks as optional enhancement, not the source of truth.
decisions.rationale: Agents must still perform the semantic Mainline workflow themselves; hooks can provide context but cannot decide goals, turns, fingerprints, or conflicts.
The durable purpose of the skill is to teach agents how to use all practical Mainline capabilities across a coding lifecycle, not just install the skill.

decisions.chose: Move the private npx skills and GOPRIVATE install details into temporary setup/distribution sections.
decisions.rationale: The private loop is useful now but should not define the long-term skill design.

rejected.alternative: Keep the previous skill focused mainly on private-repo installation and basic Mainline commands
rejected.reason: That failed to serve as a replacement for AGENTS.md and hooks, and would under-trigger for normal coding tasks.
Hooks are optional context automation and cannot replace agent judgment for starting intents, appending turns, sealing, or surfacing conflicts.

risks: The broader skill description may trigger Mainline checks more often in repositories that have partial Mainline signals, which can add setup friction if the repository has stale or incomplete Mainline configuration.

The skill now instructs agents to bootstrap CLI/setup when Mainline is needed; an agent may need to ask before initialization in a repository where writing AGENTS.md or Git refspecs would be surprising.
followups: When public CLI distribution exists, replace the private GOPRIVATE bootstrap wording with the public install path.
After testing with real skill-trigger behavior, tune the description if agents still under-trigger or over-trigger.

architectural_claims: The Mainline skill should be the primary agent-facing integration and should work even without AGENTS.md or installed hooks.
Hooks are optional enhancement/context providers rather than mandatory workflow drivers.
Private-repo distribution details are temporary scaffolding, not the main purpose of the skill.
behavioral_changes: The skill frontmatter should trigger for broader coding-agent workflows in Mainline repositories.
Agents following the skill now attempt or guide CLI setup when Mainline is needed instead of assuming the CLI already exists.
Agents following the skill now have explicit instructions for commit, seal, publish, review, and coverage rescue flows.
security_implications: The internal private install path continues to rely on existing GitHub SSH or token authorization and does not embed credentials.
migration_notes: Existing installed copies of the previous skill should be updated with npx skills update or reinstalled from this branch/source to get the broader workflow behavior.
